pattern: 3-D DP counting binary arrangements where no run of a single digit exceeds a limit, with prefix-sum optimization to avoid O(limit) inner loops.
hints: Let f0[i][j] = ways to arrange i zeros and j ones with the last digit being 0 (and f1 for last digit 1). A new run of r zeros can follow any arrangement ending in 1: f0[i][j] = sum f1[i-r][j] for r=1..min(i,limit).
Base cases: pure runs of a single digit up to length limit are valid. Set f0[r][0]=1 for r in 1..limit and f1[0][r]=1 for r in 1..limit.
Speed up the sum using running prefix sums over the first index for f1 (to compute f0) and over the second index for f0 (to compute f1). This reduces the O(limit) inner sum to O(1) per cell.

edge_inputs.description: 1 zero 1 one limit 2 - both orderings valid
1 zero 2 ones limit 1 - only one arrangement
limit 1 forces strict alternation with unequal counts
limit exactly equals total length - all C(n,ones) arrangements valid

stress.description: small random parameters so oracle brute-force finishes fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    zero = rng.randint(1, size)
    one = rng.randint(1, size)
    limit = rng.randint(1, zero + one)
    return [zero, one, limit]
